document.title: Phase Characterization of TiO 2 Powder by XRD and TEM

figure.caption: Figure 1 Figure 1 X-ray diffraction of rutile TiO 2 (a) micropowders and (b) nanopowders.

analysis_paragraphs.text: XRD measurements were performed on the Bruker D8 Advance diffractometer operating in the reflection mode with Cu-Kα radiation (35 kV, 30 mA) and diffracted beam monochromator, using a step scan mode with the step of 0.075°( 2θ) and 4 s per step. Diffraction patterns of both anatase and rutile TiO 2 powders were compared with reference to JCPDS database. Additionally, the morphology of the powder was observed by TEM with a JEOL JEM-2100 ultra high resolution TEM operating at 160 kV.
analysis_paragraphs.relevant_text: XRD measurements were performed on the Bruker D8 Advance diffractometer operating in the reflection mode with Cu-Kα radiation (35 kV, 30 mA) and diffracted beam monochromator, using a step scan mode with the step of 0.075°( 2θ) and 4 s per step. Diffraction patterns of both anatase and rutile TiO 2 powders were compared with reference to JCPDS database. Additionally, the morphology of the powder was observed by TEM with a JEOL JEM-2100 ultra high resolution TEM operating at 160 kV.

analysis_paragraphs.text: XRD patterns of nano-TiO 2 and micro-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure 1 and Figure 2 , respectively. In Figure 1 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 27°, 36° and 55° indicating TiO 2 in the rutile phase. On the other hand, in Figure 2 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 25°a nd 48° indicating TiO 2 in the anatase phase. All peaks are in good agreement with the standard spectrum (JCPDS no.: 88-1175 and 84-1286). From Figure 1 and Figure 2 , they were shown that the diffraction pattern peak intensity of the TiO 2 increases with increasing particles size. These results suggested that the nano-TiO 2 powder is composed of irregular polycrystalline. Amorphous revealed a broad pattern with low intensity; however, the effect of the amorphous materials on the broadening of the XRD patterns of nanosized TiO 2 is negligible.

Diffraction patterns of both anatase and rutile TiO 2 powders were compared with reference to JCPDS database. Additionally, the morphology of the powder was observed by TEM with a JEOL JEM-2100 ultra high resolution TEM operating at 160 kV.

XRD patterns of nano-TiO 2 and micro-TiO 2 in rutile and anatase phases are shown in Figure 1 and Figure 2 , respectively. In Figure 1 , XRD patterns exhibited strong diffraction peaks at 27°, 36° and 55° indicating TiO 2 in the rutile phase. From Figure 1 and Figure 2 , they were shown that the diffraction pattern peak intensity of the TiO 2 increases with increasing particles size.
